notes: This pass again used the upgraded flow: tiny-veneer scan first, then bank-aware caller checks across 1AF0..1BFF before choosing the next seam.
The leading byte at 1AF0 is RTL, but unlike the earlier 1880 stub it only has weak caller context from unresolved higher-bank C3, so it is not promoted into a real return stub label.

The busiest raw target in this pocket is 1BF7, which receives multiple same-bank JMP hits from later unresolved C3 regions; however the local bytes around 1BF7 still do not stabilize into a trustworthy entry body under cautious inspection, so that pocket is not promoted.

1C00 is the next better candidate seam: it has an incoming same-bank raw caller and the local byte structure beginning at 1C00 reads more coherently than the noisy 1AF0..1BFF lead-in, so the seam is advanced there rather than forcing a shaky split earlier.
